ragChunks.title: Tax compliance review SOP
ragChunks.text: Audit officers must treat AI-generated compliance deviations as prioritization signals only. A case requires human review, source verification, evidence quality checks, and a citizen correction window before escalation. Reports must include a watermark, evidence identifiers, policy citations, and a warning that the score does not prove fraud.

ragChunks.title: Vehicle and asset signal guidance
ragChunks.text: Luxury vehicles, high engine capacity registrations, and high-value residential or commercial property can be reviewed when materially inconsistent with declared income. Asset signals must be correlated with ownership dates, filing behavior, utility consumption, and business relationships before any enforcement recommendation.

ragChunks.title: Citizen correction and appeal policy
ragChunks.text: Citizens must receive a safe explanation that avoids investigative tactics and does not expose peer comparisons. Correction submissions should allow stale ownership, outdated utility records, incorrect business links, and already-declared income evidence. Human reviewers must consider correction evidence before closure.

ragChunks.title: Business ownership income review note
ragChunks.text: Business directorship, shareholder, and partnership relationships should be compared with declared income, tax paid, filer status, and asset purchases. A business relationship alone is not a violation; it is a review signal when paired with undeclared or materially inconsistent economic activity.
